title: chrysothamnus nauseosus var. bigelovii
intro: Chrysothamnus nauseosus var. bigelovii, also known as Bigelow's rabbitbrush, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the western United States and Canada. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Bigelow's rabbitbrush include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and act as an antioxidant, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds. In herbal preparations, Bigelow's rabbitbrush can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
